assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will 'automatically upgrade to the next available version in the Regular channel' and specifically mentions 1.31

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response states Extended channel clusters 'Continue receiving security patches for up to 24 months' and mentions they have '~12-18 months' to plan

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states No Channel clusters 'No automatic upgrades will occur' - it doesn't mention that EoS triggers force upgrades for both control plane and node pools

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: No mention of maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer EoS force-upgrades for 30 days

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response provides a detailed preparation strategy with timeline recommendations and emphasizes testing compatibility before auto-upgrades occur

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: No mention of Cloud Logging or EoS notifications as planning tools

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: No mention of the deprecation insights dashboard for API compatibility checking

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: While the response mentions security risks for the No Channel cluster, it doesn't specifically warn against 'snowflaking' or manually freezing versions as a practice to avoid
